summary: Generates procedural noise patterns (Perlin, Simplex, Sparse, etc.) as a texture. Commonly used for driving displacements, masks, or generative visual effects.

key_params.name: type
key_params.note: Noise algorithm: Perlin, Simplex, Sparse, Alligator, etc.

key_params.name: amp
key_params.note: Overall amplitude/brightness of the noise output

key_params.name: period
key_params.note: Spatial frequency of noise — smaller values yield coarser patterns

key_params.name: t
key_params.note: Time offset for animating noise; link to absTime.seconds for smooth motion

key_params.name: monochrome
key_params.note: When on, outputs greyscale noise; off gives per-channel RGB noise

key_params.name: outputderivatives
key_params.note: 2025.30000+: emit Simplex/Perlin 4D derivatives (gradient) alongside the value — extra channels can drive normals or flow directly

common_gotchas: Forgetting to animate the 't' parameter results in a static image — wire absTime.seconds or a CHOP to it.
Resolution defaults to 256x256; set it explicitly or use an input to inherit resolution.
2025.30000+: the new 4D derivative output is GPU-cheap and avoids manually finite-differencing the noise downstream.
